saint-germain-des-pres.name: Saint-Germain-des-Prés
saint-germain-des-pres.summary: Home base every June — the bookshops, the boulevard terraces, and the streets between the Seine and Sciences Po.
saint-germain-des-pres.essay: Every trip starts and ends here. The apartment on Rue Dauphine sits in a quiet courtyard a few steps from Pont Neuf, and the quarter unfolds from there: breakfast on the Café de Flore terrace, afternoons drifting past the bookshops off Place de l'Odéon, coffee at Café Bismuth when the day needs a pause. The last walk through Saint-Germain before leaving has become a ritual of its own — the same streets, a year older each time.
saint-germain-des-pres.personalHistory: Studied at Sciences Po, just west of the quarter, during the 2009 study-abroad semester — the reason every Paris trip since has been anchored to this side of the river.

montmartre.name: Montmartre
montmartre.summary: The hilltop village — Sacré-Cœur's panorama, La Maison Rose, and the last working vineyard in Paris.
montmartre.essay: In June 2026 the heat did what nothing else can: it emptied the steps of Sacré-Cœur. The climb rewards either way — the whole city laid out below the basilica — but the better half of Montmartre is the walk down, past the pink façade of La Maison Rose on Rue de l'Abreuvoir and the old vineyard behind it, on streets that still feel like the village the hill used to be.

ile-saint-louis.name: Île Saint-Louis
ile-saint-louis.summary: The smaller island — moules-frites on the quayside, street jazz, and the Berthillon queue.
ile-saint-louis.essay: The island earns its afternoon slowly. Lunch was moules-frites at a quayside table while a street jazz band worked the crowd — better than any concert hall — and then the short walk to Berthillon, where the queue is part of the ritual. Two scoops, the stone lip of the quay, and the river doing the rest.

menton-old-town.name: Old Town
menton-old-town.summary: Stepped lanes stacked above the sea — the rampes, the basilica parvis, and Italy visible down the coast.
menton-old-town.essay: Menton's old town is vertical: everything is a climb or a descent. Up the Rampes Saint-Michel to the parvis, where the basilica hangs over the sea and Italy sits down the coast at sunset. The better version came the next morning — out before six into the blue hour, the lanes empty, the shutters closed, the whole hillside briefly private before coming back down the rampes by half past six.

krakow-old-town.name: Old Town
krakow-old-town.summary: Home for the Kraków leg — a flat off the Rynek, long lunches, and Kanonicza at dusk.
krakow-old-town.essay: Kraków was lived from inside the Old Town: a flat just off the Rynek, which turns the whole quarter into an extension of the apartment. A slow first morning after the long train day from Berlin, Mały Rynek on the loop back, dinner in the lanes, and Kanonicza — the street of canons running down toward Wawel — at dusk. The last morning was just coffee and the bags, which is its own kind of compliment.

le-marais.name: Le Marais
le-marais.summary: Courtyards, falafel lines, and — on the day of the Fête de la Musique — a stage on every corner.
le-marais.essay: So far the Marais belongs to one afternoon: June 21st, the Fête de la Musique, when every courtyard and street corner in the quarter becomes a stage and the crowd drifts from band to band. One visit is not a claim to know the place — this page will grow.
